title: Add Gruvbox to theme switcher dropdown
body:
## Summary

- Added `.gruvbox` to the CSS selector in `variables.css` so the theme applies when selected by name
- Added a custom `index.hbs` template (based on mdBook 0.5.2 default) with a "Gruvbox" button in the theme switcher list
- Updated `main.rs` to install `index.hbs` and set `default-theme`/`preferred-dark-theme` to `"gruvbox"` in `book.toml`

## Problem

After running `mdbook-gruvbox install`, the theme works but "Gruvbox" doesn't appear in the theme switcher dropdown — only the built-in themes (Light, Rust, Coal, Navy, Ayu) are listed. Since the CSS overrides all of them, every option looks the same and there's no indication the user is on a custom theme.

## How it works

mdBook's theme switcher JS dynamically discovers themes from `<button class="theme">` elements in the HTML. By providing a custom `index.hbs` with a Gruvbox button (`id="mdbook-theme-gruvbox"`), the JS picks it up automatically — no JS changes needed. The `.gruvbox` CSS selector ensures the variables apply when the `<html>` element gets `class="gruvbox"`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `cargo build` compiles clean
- [ ] `mdbook-gruvbox install` on a fresh mdBook project writes `index.hbs` to the theme dir
- [ ] `mdbook build` produces a book with "Gruvbox" in the theme dropdown
- [ ] Selecting "Gruvbox" from the dropdown applies the gruvbox colors
- [ ] Built-in themes still apply gruvbox colors (existing behavior preserved)
